title: Water Main Repair
desc: We provide professional water main repair services for homes and properties across the GTA. If you are experiencing low water pressure, leaks, or water line damage, our team quickly diagnoses the issue and performs safe, effective repairs. We handle underground water line repairs and replacements with minimal disruption to your property, restoring reliable water flow and protecting your home from further damage.

## Signs Your Water Main Line May Need Repair or Replacement

![Repairing Buried Pipe](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/5c3635744576443f81df346edb7cc3e1.jpg/v1/fill/w_391,h_391,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Repairing%20Buried%20Pipe.jpg)

![Plumber Fixing Sink](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_7a4e33a8178f4212a7b91f0441c6d1cb~mv2.jpeg/v1/fill/w_391,h_391,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Plumber%20Fixing%20Sink.jpeg)

01

Low Water Pressure Throughout Your Home\
A sudden drop in water pressure across multiple fixtures may indicate a clog, corrosion buildup, or a leak in your water main line. Addressing the problem early can help prevent more serious plumbing issues and costly damage.

02

Unexpectedly High Water Bills\
If you notice a sudden increase in your water bill without any change in usage, it may be caused by a hidden leak in the main water line. A professional inspection can quickly identify the source and prevent ongoing water loss.

03

Water Pooling on Your Property\
Unexplained puddles, soggy patches in your yard, or damp areas near your driveway or basement could signal a break in your underground water main. Prompt repairs are essential to prevent structural damage and further water loss.

04

Discoloured or Contaminated Water\
Rusty, cloudy, or foul-smelling water coming from your taps may indicate corrosion, sediment buildup, or contamination within the water main line. This can affect the quality and safety of your household water supply and should be inspected immediately.
